title: Backend Design
use_when: Design backend architecture for implementation with API contracts, data model changes, service boundaries, security, and observability.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Backend Design when the request is mainly market strategy, research synthesis, finance modeling, or executive communication; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Backend Design UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: Backend Design fits AI Execution work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs Backend Design UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Backend Design UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Backend Design as generic AI Execution advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Backend Design when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $backend-design to turn this backend context into a decision-ready Backend Design UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: Backend Design UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
